meta_title: For Founders: marketing automation to Solve no project ideas (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to marketing automation and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Marketing Automation to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Stop guessing. Learn how to build high-margin marketing automation tools to solve the 'no project ideas' trap using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Stop searching for 'ideas' and start mining market inefficiencies. By building automated marketing scrapers and content analyzers, you identify high-demand pain points in real-time. Use Antigravity to scaffold your infrastructure and Claude Code to iterate your business logic, turning raw data into a validated, revenue-generating SaaS product.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank repository, waiting for a lightning bolt of inspiration that never comes. The 'no project ideas' paralysis is the graveyard of potential founders; it’s not a lack of creativity, it’s a lack of market signal. While you wait for a perfect vision, your competitors are already extracting value from the chaos of the internet.

True innovation isn't about inventing a new problem; it's about automating the solutions to existing, expensive ones. By deploying an automated marketing intelligence engine, you stop guessing and start observing where money is already flowing. This is the shift from 'wantrepreneur' to operator.

steps.title: Define the Market Signal
steps.description: Identify a niche by scraping platform discussions (e.g., Reddit, Twitter) to find recurring complaints. We will build a detector that logs recurring keyword clusters.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a Python scraper that monitors niche subreddits for 'how do I' or 'alternative to' queries and saves them to a structured JSON database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Create a data schema for storing extracted social media pain points including timestamp, source_url, and sentiment_score.'

steps.title: Automate Content Analysis
steps.description: Pipe the scraped data into an LLM to categorize the intent. We need to distinguish between 'noisy complaints' and 'commercial intent'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Integrate an OpenAI API client to process scraped text and output a JSON object containing the 'problem_category' and 'willingness_to_pay_score'.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add-feature --prompt 'Implement an async function to categorize raw text using gpt-4o-mini and save the results to a sqlite database.'

steps.title: Build the Notification Layer
steps.description: Don't just store data; alert yourself. Build an automated email or Slack trigger that notifies you when a high-intent problem is detected.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a notification service that sends a summary of high-scoring problems to a Slack webhook every 24 hours.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --command 'npm install @slack/webhook' --prompt 'Write a script to format the daily summary and send it to the configured webhook.'

steps.title: Validate via Landing Page
steps.description: Once a trend is identified, deploy a landing page to capture emails before building the full solution.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a responsive landing page template using Tailwind CSS that highlights the discovered problem and includes a waitlist signup form.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ui --framework 'nextjs' --prompt 'Create a landing page component that accepts emails and saves them to a Supabase table.'

steps.title: Iterate with Claude Code
steps.description: Use Claude Code to refine your backend based on user feedback from your waitlist.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the backend to support user authentication and priority queueing for potential customers.
steps.claude_code_command: claude refactor --prompt 'Convert existing prototype logic into a production-ready API service with error handling and logging.'

pro_tips: Never build a tool for 'everyone'. If your automation doesn't narrow down to a specific, high-spending persona, it will fail.
Use the 'Pain-to-Revenue' ratio: Only build if the problem you've found is costing the user at least $500/month.
Don't build a database schema first. Use SQLite with Antigravity to stay lean until you have 100+ active users.
Use your own automation to find your first 10 customers by commenting on the very threads you are scraping.
Optimize for 'Time-to-First-Dollar' rather than code elegance. Ship the MVP in under 48 hours.
content.next_steps: After identifying your first validated problem, pivot from data collection to building the core solution. Use the email list you generated to conduct 10 customer interviews before writing a single line of production code.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools and the framework to move from 'no ideas' to a validated MVP. Stop overthinking the build and start engineering the outcome. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
